title: Harden strict Work Item closure validation

scenarioCoverage.scenario: unmerged or mismatched PR blocks closure

riskAssessment.reason: Closure must not delete branches or synchronize the base when archived evidence or merge identity is invalid.

executionDecision.reason: Closure validation has explicit fail-closed boundaries and existing lifecycle tests.

preReviewWarnings: Review that closure performs no destructive cleanup before all evidence and PR checks pass.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Closure validates new archived evidence with strict Contract/Summary rules.
Closure remains fail-closed before branch deletion or base synchronization.
Existing lifecycle regression tests pass and cover mismatched PR/base conditions.
Closure order and postconditions are documented.
guidelines: Validate archived evidence strictly before destructive cleanup.
Keep closure postconditions explicit and testable.

intent.problem: Closure currently validates archived evidence through the legacy compatibility path, which can allow newly archived records to bypass strict checks before branch cleanup.
constraints: Preserve legacy parsing only where explicitly required.
Do not change branch deletion order.
intent.rationale: Closure is the irreversible lifecycle step and must use the strongest evidence validation available.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
